message: agent1: implementing feat(mux) support http.Request.PathValue in Go 1.22. Plan to add new files path_value.go and path_value_fallback.go, and add a small hook call in mux.go's ServeHTTP (routeHTTP) method. Will try to minimize changes to mux.go to avoid conflicts. Let me know what you're working on.

agent2: implementing feat(mux) route hit monitoring/metrics. Will touch mux.go (add metricsCollector field + SetMetricsCollector + timing in routeHTTP), and add new files metrics.go, metrics_test.go. Will try to minimize changes to mux.go and avoid clobbering. Let me know what you're working on.

agent1: my only mux.go change will be in routeHTTP() (mux.go ~line 456-458): after 'if _, _, h := mx.tree.FindRoute(...); h != nil {' I'll insert a single line 'setPathValue(rctx, r)' before 'h.ServeHTTP(w, r)'. New files path_value.go (+build go1.22) and path_value_fallback.go (+build !go1.22) define setPathValue(rctx *Context, r *http.Request). Should be a minimal, non-overlapping diff with your timing instrumentation as long as you wrap around h.ServeHTTP rather than replacing that block. Let me know if that works for you.

agent2: ack. I'm adding metrics timing in the same block. To keep it simple for both of us, I'll keep a single 'h.ServeHTTP(w, r)' call site (no branching) and wrap timing around it with time.Now()/time.Since() before/after that single call, then call mx.recordHit(...) conditionally after. So your single-line setPathValue(rctx, r) insertion right before h.ServeHTTP(w, r) will still work fine. I'll paste my exact diff for that block shortly.

agent2: done. mux.go routeHTTP now has exactly one 'h.ServeHTTP(w, r)' call site inside 'if _, _, h := mx.tree.FindRoute(rctx, method, routePath); h != nil {' block (around line 468-477). Insert your 'setPathValue(rctx, r)' line directly before that single h.ServeHTTP(w, r) call and it won't conflict with my timing code. My other changes are additive (new metricsCollector field on Mux struct, new SetMetricsCollector method, new recordHit method, new metrics.go file) so should be safe. Go ahead.
agent1: done, no conflicts on my end — mux.go diff is a single added line 'setPathValue(rctx, r)' right before h.ServeHTTP(w, r) in routeHTTP. Wrote patch.txt. Good luck with metrics.
agent2: metrics feature complete and tests passing, patch.txt written with mux.go/metrics.go/metrics_test.go changes. No further mux.go edits planned on my end.
